Updates `flask` from 3.0.0 to 3.1.3

author: Johannes Segitz
message:
Set SELinux type for secret file explicitly

Since the file is changed by creating a temporary file and then
atomically renaming it the normal file handling rules of SELinux
don't work properly. Therefor set the SELinux type explicitly on
the temporary file.

See https://bugzilla.suse.com/show_bug.cgi?id=1252092 for more details

author: Thomas Habets
message:
Up version to 1.11

Notable changes:
* Change secret key bits to be 160 instead of 128 bits (#266 / #271)
* Add support for black&white terminals (#268 / #270)
* Fix `grace_period` for IPv6 link-local addresses (#265)

author: flowers9
message:
add support for B&W terminals (#270)

Use current colors to display the qr code by default rather than using black on grey, as we don't actually use white/black printing, just whatever the current foreground and background are (and swap them as needed) and we're printing a border in any case. This allows printing to monochrome terminals by default, where black on grey would translate to black on black.  The old default is available as options to -Q (ANSIg, UTF8g).  Inverted colors are available with ANSIi and UTF8i.

Explicitly make ANSI the default if no option is chosen.

Renamed a few defines to better reflect the actual codes (inverse on/off reverse white/black), changed a couple of for loop variables to be consistent (using x for column position).  Also changed isBlack to isInverted to match the define name changes.

Added a reset to the start of the output to make sure no pre-existing settings messed with the output, and removed the inverse off code from the default ansi color setup as this was no longer needed.

Moved from printf/puts to fputs (with additional \n's as needed) for qr code output as no output was formatted anyway.

Update google-authenticator.c

author: Thomas Habets
message:
Up version to 1.10

Notable changes:
* Shorten syslog name to work with rsyslog (#172)
* Update config file with grace period in  all cases (#193)
* Remove printing QR code using Google Charts URL (it's turned down)

author: Thomas Habets
message:
Remove printing QR code using Google Charts URL

Google Charts URLs has been deprecated since 2012, and is finally
being turned off.

The remaining options for getting secrets into the app are:

1. Have `libqrencode` installed, generating a QR code locally and
   displaying it on the terminal.
2. Enter the secret manually via the text representation shown.

Fixes #244

author: mugglewei
message:
set variable 'buffer' that in base32_decode to unsigned integer type

In base32_decode, variable 'buffer' is signed integer may lead undefined
behavior when left operand.
